Off Calendar Mar-14-2023 Text Ruling

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-13-2022 Set For Trial On May-01-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Mpas In Support Of Motion To Order Sequence/Timing Of Discovery Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar May-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JOHN FITZPATRICK VANNUCCI, Esq. of Law Offices of John Fitzpatrick Vannucci (Tel: 415-981-7500) for plaintiff;
Atty. ARI HERSHER, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw LLP (Tel: 415-397-2823) for defendant Qiagen, LLC.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF REINA KARUNARATNE's Motion To Order Sequence/Timing Of Discovery Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Protective Order.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Qiagen's motion to compel production of the at-issue voicemail is granted; the voicemail shall be produced by May 23, 2022. Reina Karunaratne's motion to control the sequence/timing of discovery or, in the alternative, for protective order is denied. The judge pro tem's report and recommendation is adopted. No sanctions are awarded.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and attaches a copy of the report and recommendation.

Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Mar-22-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT QIAGEN, INC.'S Motion To Strike.

Defendant QIAGEN, LLC's motion to strike is denied. The allegations regarding the prior suit add needed background to the amended complaint. While the court agrees that the level of detail that plaintiff pleads was unnecessary, the allegations are not necessarily improper per CCP 436.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling order in compliance with CRC3.1312.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT QIAGEN, LLC's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint.

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Qiagen, LLC's "demurrer to amended complaint" is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer may be filed. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant's response is now due by February 17, 2022.
